reasoning: VIX at 30.67 is in the extreme fear zone (>25), though direction is flat (-0.26% change — fear is stable, not accelerating). Overnight gap up +2.97 is bullish, but sector rotation is strongly risk-off: Tech (XLK) -1.22%, Financials (XLF) -2.01%, while only Energy (XLE) +1.67% is positive. This rotation pattern and high VIX regime favor bearish directional setups (premium cheaper on volatility, higher-probability reversals). No major macro events today removes binary risk. Early premarket ribbon is BULL stacked but spread is only 8 cents (session noise). Bias leans bearish due to elevated premium cost + sector weakness, though gap up + stable VIX direction prevent stronger conviction.
key_observations: VIX 30.67 = extreme fear regime (>25); premium expensive for bull directional, favorable for bear directional
VIX direction flat (-0.26%) = fear is stable, not building further; no sharp compression expected but no spike either
Overnight gap +2.97 significant, but sector rotation is weak: Tech -1.22%, Financials -2.01% (major cap outflows), Energy sole bright spot +1.67%
Risk-off rotation dominates macro sentiment despite gap up; suggests market absorbed fear and is rotating defensively, not rallying sustainably
No macro events today (calendar empty for 2026-03-30); removes hard no-trade windows but also removes catalyst for volatility reset
Ribbon BULL stack (+8 cents spread) is early-session noise; too tight to trust directional bias; wait for 09:30 RTH open to confirm structure
